summary: Policy-backed control for whether the Sleep option is shown in the power menu. Useful for managed or simplified menus, but it removes a common quick-sleep shortcut.

setting.name: Show Sleep Option
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show or hide Sleep in the power menu. This policy decides whether that shortcut is visible.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide it when they want a simpler menu, when sleep should not be offered, or when the device should use other shutdown behaviors instead.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding Sleep removes a quick low-power option from the menu, which can simplify the UI but also make sleep less accessible.
setting.professional_notes: WindowsExplorer.admx maps ShowSleepOption under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. WindowsExplorer.adml says enabled shows Sleep if the hardware supports it, disabled hides it, and not configured lets users choose through Power Options Control Panel.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force Sleep visibility and users can choose through the normal controls.

allowed_values.label: Sleep option hidden
allowed_values.meaning: The Sleep option is never shown in the power menu.

allowed_values.label: Sleep option shown
allowed_values.meaning: The Sleep option is shown in the power menu when the hardware supports it.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes ShowSleepOption = 0, which matches the documented hide behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 8 and later systems exposing the Explorer policy
windows_defaults.notes: Not configured leaves Sleep visibility under the normal Windows controls.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Managed systems that want Sleep hidden
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep the normal Sleep visibility behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Sleep removed from the power menu.
tradeoffs: Sleep can remain visible where supported.
No forced menu change is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide sleep menu entry
intended_for: Systems that should not advertise Sleep in the power menu
avoid_for: Users who want quick access to Sleep

states.rationale: Hide the Sleep option from the power menu.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a simpler power menu or do not want users choosing Sleep from that menu.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Sleep to stay easy to access from the power menu.
tradeoffs: The Sleep shortcut disappears from the menu.
Users need another path if they still want Sleep.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsExplorer.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps ShowSleepOption under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsExplorer.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy shows Sleep when supported, disabling it hides Sleep, and not configuring it leaves the choice to the user through the normal controls.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes ShowSleepOption = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - ShowSleepOption policy
evidence.summary: WindowsExplorer.admx shows ShowSleepOption under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy shows Sleep in the power options menu, disabling it never shows Sleep, and not configuring it leaves the user able to choose via Power Options Control Panel. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe - ShowSleepOption 0/1 toggle
evidence.summary: The guest probe wrote ShowSleepOption=0 and ShowSleepOption=1, queried the live registry after each write, opened the Power Options surface twice, and restored the original state.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact policy surface, value semantics, and user-facing behavior. The app writes the documented hide state.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="ShowSleepOption" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="ShowSleepOption">; WindowsExplorer.adml: If you disable this policy setting, the sleep option will never be shown in the Power Options menu.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that disabling the policy hides Sleep from the Power Options menu. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-showsleepoption-admx. Win25H2Clean reversible probe at research/evidence-files/vm-tooling-staging/power_hide_sleep_option_probe.txt confirmed writes for 0 and 1, live registry queries after each write, Power Options opening in both states, and restoration to the original state.
